title: desmanthus caudatus
intro: Desmanthus caudatus, also known as desert holly or prairie hollyhock, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with roots and leaves used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Desmanthus caudatus are said to provide relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, and may also have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying and infusing the leaves, roots, and flowers in hot water or oil.
